FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 2 assessment (2018–2020), N=9,008 adolescents

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; cross-sectional linear regression examining associations between problematic social media use and alcohol expectancies

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed research article in BMC Public Health (2023)

FT_IC2.evidence: Qualifying SM variable: problematic social media use measured via Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ); paired as IV with individual-trait-level DV (alcohol expectancies—a within-person belief construct)

FT_IC3.evidence: Inferential analysis with multiple numeric results: B=0.045 (95% CI 0.020–0.069, p<0.001) for positive alcohol expectancies; B=0.072 (95% CI 0.043–0.101, p<0.001) for negative alcohol expectancies (Model 3)

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric results reported for SM↔outcome relationship across main models

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional, not longitudinal; EC7 does not apply
notes: Paper includes both time on social media and problematic social media use as predictors; only problematic use showed significant associations with alcohol expectancies in adjusted models

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Baseline and Year 2 (2018–2020), N=9,673 adolescents who used screens

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; prospective generalized logistic mixed effects models and generalized linear effects models examining associations between ACEs and problematic screen use

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed research article in BMC Public Health (2023)

FT_IC2.evidence: Qualifying screen use variables: Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ) as IV; problematic mobile phone use via Mobile Phone Involvement Questionnaire (MPIQ) as secondary outcome; both paired with individual-trait-level DV (ACEs—within-person trauma exposure)

FT_IC3.evidence: Inferential analysis with multiple numeric results: logistic model for problematic social media use (ACE 4+ OR=1.52, 95% CI 0.83–2.79); secondary linear models showing associations between ACEs and continuous problematic use scores for video game, social media, and mobile phone use

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric results reported in main and supplemental models across binary and continuous problematic screen use outcomes

FT_EC7_reason: Design is longitudinal (baseline → Year 2). Paper measures SM use (SMAQ, MPIQ) as DV in analyses. Analyses treat screen use outcomes as dependent variables; no analyses treat SM/phone as IV predicting other variables. Therefore EC7 applies—paper excluded under FT-EC7
notes: Paper analyzes prospective associations where ACE exposure at baseline predicts problematic screen use outcomes at Year 2. SM/phone use is consistently the DV, not IV

FT_IC1.evidence: Special issue introduction referencing ABCD Study; states empirical reports are based on ABCD data (baseline, Years 1 and 2 assessments)

FT_IC5.evidence: Non-empirical paper; special issue introduction/editorial that reviews and contextualizes other empirical papers but does not report original analyses of ABCD data

notes: This is a special issue introduction written by consortium members describing the scope and context of empirical papers in the issue. It does not conduct original analyses
